metadata.source_file: /home/jonaswsl/projects/daygame-coach/data/06h.DET.confidence-propagation/daily_evolution/Advice For Your First Cold Approach EVER [L9mcyTuXYjc]/Advice For Your First Cold Approach EVER [L9mcyTuXYjc].audio.asr.clean16k.conversations.json

video_title: Advice For Your First Cold Approach EVER

video_type.reasoning: Zero live approaches. All approach examples are hypothetical demonstrations. SPEAKER_01 dominates >90% of segments. While SPEAKER_00 contributes briefly (mastermind call format), the content is essentially a single coach lecturing on first approach mindset.

SPEAKER_01.reasoning: Dominant speaker teaching approach concepts, giving examples, referencing filmed infield, directing mastermind call. Uses instructional language throughout.

SPEAKER_00.reasoning: Mastermind participant who suggested the video topic and contributes brief responses. Shares anecdote about talking to a girl (segments 74-76). Receives teaching, not delivering it.

UNKNOWN.reasoning: Brief interjection from another mastermind participant (segments 19-21). Responds to coach's question about what opener they'd use. Very limited speaking time.

enrichments.description: Introduction and framing the topic: mindset for a first approach ever or after a long break
Best opener for beginners is the easiest one you can actually do — direct or indirect

techniques_discussed.example: Do you happen to know if there is a Starbucks here in this mall?
Excuse me, I saw you over here, you're really cute, I wanted to come say hello

enrichments.description: Permission to bail: indirect openers let anxious beginners exit gracefully, reducing pressure

techniques_discussed.example: Do you go to the Planet Fitness on Main Street?
Permission to bail — you can just say okay no problem, have a good day

enrichments.description: Basic conversation structure after opening: where she lives, work, local spots, then suggest dinner

techniques_discussed.example: A little story about how you've only lived here for two years
What about you, do you like it?

enrichments.description: Student validates: imperfect approaches are fine, women respect men who try despite nervousness

techniques_discussed.example: She can feel when a guy's just being nice and wants to talk

enrichments.description: Practice somewhere unfamiliar for anonymity so rejection has no social consequences
Outcome independence: any conversation with an attractive woman is a victory regardless of result

techniques_discussed.example: You have to be completely unattached from the outcome
